messages.content: rewrite, "Inspections and JSA’s are being submitted as required, however capturing more data is necessary to obtain better monthly and year end analytics. Including pictures on passed and failed items, assigning corrective actions, documenting good and bad observations will provide more substance in submitted inspections."

To improve monthly and year-end analytics, it is essential to collect additional data beyond the current requirements of submitting inspections and Job Safety Analysis (JSA's). One way to achieve this is by incorporating photographs of both passed and failed items. In addition, assigning corrective actions and documenting both positive and negative observations will greatly enhance the depth and quality of the inspections being submitted.

rewrite, "Submitted Hand and Power Tool Checklists, verify that tools are in good working condition, being used by trained/authorized employees, and used with appropriate PPE. Manuals are maintained on site, and at the head office service centre"

The submitted Hand and Power Tool Checklists ensure that tools are in proper working order, used by trained and authorized employees, and used with the appropriate personal protective equipment (PPE). Furthermore, manuals for these tools are not only kept on-site but also at the service centre located in the head office.

rewrite, "All specialized work requiring use of heavy machinery is mainly performed by sub trades. Heavy machinery inspections are maintained and kept on site. These inspections are conducted by authorized, trained, and certified personnel. Training records have been verified by site superintendent and/or safety officers. "

Sub trades are predominantly responsible for carrying out specialized work involving the use of heavy machinery. Regular inspections of the heavy machinery are conducted and the inspection documents are kept on-site. These inspections are performed by personnel who are authorized, trained, and certified for the purpose. The training records of these personnel have been duly verified by the site superintendent and/or safety officers.

rewrite, "Based off submitted inspections and site visits, DC3 & HOOP were the only projects utilizing heavy machinery operated by Urbacon employees. Heavy machinery is inspected prior to use and operated by competent and qualified personnel."

After reviewing the submitted inspections and conducting site visits, it was determined that only the DC3 and HOOP projects required the use of heavy machinery operated by Urbacon employees. Before operation, thorough inspections are conducted on the heavy machinery, and it is operated by personnel who are competent and qualified in handling such equipment.

rewrite, "Records indicate that monthly JHSC workplace inspections are being submitted from all required projects, and regular meetings are being held. Inspections indicate access and egress routes are clear, and fire extinguishers are inspected monthly and in working order. Manuals for various office equipment such as copiers, laminators, electric hole punchers, etc. are available for reference & found at the service centre. No recommendations made to management this year"

According to the records, monthly Joint Health and Safety Committee (JHSC) workplace inspections are being submitted for all mandated projects, accompanied by regular meetings. These inspections have revealed that access and egress routes are free from obstructions, and the fire extinguishers are inspected monthly to ensure they are in proper working condition. Manuals for various office equipment, such as copiers, laminators, and electric hole punchers, are readily available for reference and can be found at the service centre. It is noteworthy that no recommendations have been made to the management this year based on the inspections conducted.

rewrite, "Ladder inspections, Jobsite Inspection Checklists confirm workers are utilizing the adequate ladders for work activity. During JSA’s, workers are reminded to inspect ladders prior to use and report any damages to their supervisor."

To ensure the safety of workers, ladder inspections are conducted, and Jobsite Inspection Checklists are utilized to confirm that appropriate ladders are being used for the respective work activities. Furthermore, during Job Safety Analysis (JSA's), workers are reminded of the importance of inspecting ladders before use and are encouraged to report any damages or issues to their supervisor. This proactive approach helps to maintain a safe working environment and prevent potential accidents or injuries.

rewrite, "Records of Ladder inspections, Jobsite Inspection Checklists confirm workers are utilizing the adequate ladders for work activity. During JSA’s, workers are reminded to inspect ladders prior to use and report any damages to their supervisor."

The records of ladder inspections and Jobsite Inspection Checklists indicate that workers are using suitable ladders for their respective work activities. Additionally, during Job Safety Analysis (JSA's), workers receive reminders to inspect ladders before use and promptly report any damages to their supervisor. This proactive approach ensures that workers are utilizing safe and appropriate equipment while minimizing the risk of accidents or injuries.

reerite, "AMBR is the only project which requires use of scaffolding. Subcontractor inspects their scaffold on a weekly basis and submits inspections to site supervisor. Urbacon also inspects subcontractors scaffold, any deficiencies found are communicated to the contractor immediately upon discovery  and corrective action is taken immediately"

Among all the projects, only the AMBR project requires the use of scaffolding. The subcontractor responsible for the scaffolding conducts weekly inspections and submits the inspection reports to the site supervisor. Additionally, Urbacon also inspects the subcontractor's scaffold and promptly communicates any identified deficiencies to the contractor upon discovery. Immediate corrective action is then taken to rectify the issues and ensure the safety and integrity of the scaffolding.

rewrite, "Upon review of submitted vehicle inspections, not every employee who operates a company vehicle is submitting monthly inspections via Procore, however vehicle service maintenance is completed as per manufacturers guidelines; records of service is maintained by Fleet Manager."

After reviewing the submitted vehicle inspections, it has been observed that not all employees who operate company vehicles are submitting monthly inspections through Procore. However, it is important to note that vehicle service maintenance is consistently carried out in accordance with the manufacturers' guidelines. The records of these service maintenance activities are diligently maintained by the Fleet Manager.

rewrite, "Electric heater malfunctioned causing spark and small flame. Heater was positioned adjacent to loading dock washroom. No injuries or damage to building or construction materials. Black and McDonald electrician shut off hard wired power source. Urbacon Supervision extinguished small flame."

A malfunction occurred with an electric heater, resulting in a spark and a small flame. The heater had been placed near the loading dock washroom. Fortunately, no injuries or damage to the building or construction materials were reported. The on-site electrician from Black and McDonald promptly shut off the hard-wired power source, while the Urbacon Supervision team took immediate action to extinguish the small flame.

rewrite, "Sentrex CI, under contract and direction of BGIS, working on north exterior between building and sidewalk, contacted and damaged a water line. Locates for water line were not accurate. City notified via call to 311 to have water shut off and line repaired.
Upon direction of BGIS, all work in building was stopped as a safety concern and trades sent home until water issue resolved.  URBACON staff remained on site to supervise and make safe
"

While working on the north exterior area between the building and sidewalk, Sentrex CI, under the contract and direction of BGIS, accidentally contacted and damaged a water line. It was discovered that the locates for the water line were not accurate. In response, a call was made to the city's 311 service to request the water to be shut off and the line to be repaired.

Following the instructions of BGIS, all work inside the building was halted due to safety concerns, and the trades were sent home until the water issue could be resolved. However, URBACON staff remained on-site to supervise the situation and ensure that necessary safety measures were in place.
